QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_64

question.text: A stock is priced at Rs. 400. Using continuous compounding with r = 10%, q = 3%, and T = 180 days, what is the fair futures price? (Given: e^0.0345 = 1.0351)

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Step-by-step calculation: F = S x e^((r-q) x T/365) F = 400 x e^((0.10 - 0.03) x 180/365) F = 400 x e^(0.07 x 0.4932) F = 400 x e^(0.03452) F = 400 x 1.0351 (given) F = Rs. 414.04
**The Trap:** Rs. 428 uses r = 7% for full year (400 x 1.07). Rs. 400 ignores carry. Rs. 407 uses only half the rate or incorrect time fraction.
**Cross-Exam Context:** When the exam provides the value of e^x, use it directly. Series VIII may test with or without the given exponential value.
**Key Takeaway:** F = 400 x e^(0.07 x 180/365) = 400 x e^(0.0345) = 400 x 1.0351 = Rs. 414.04.
